sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Osasuna have struggled in their last five matches, managing only one win (a 5-0 cup victory) and suffering three losses, including narrow defeats to strong sides like Atletico Madrid and Sevilla. Real Sociedad, on the other hand, are unbeaten in their last six matches across all competitions, securing two wins and four draws, with a notable 3-2 victory over Athletic Club showcasing their attacking intent.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Real Sociedad have dominated Osasuna, winning both matches convincingly with a combined score of 6-1. Historically, in the last five head-to-heads, Sociedad have won three times, while Osasuna managed two victories, indicating a competitive but recently one-sided rivalry.

sections.subtitle: Team News and Key Players
sections.text: Osasuna will miss key midfielder Jon Moncayola, which could affect their midfield stability, but Ante Budimir remains a threat up front with 4 goals this season. Real Sociedad are without winger Ander Barrenetxea and defender Igor Zubeldia, but Mikel Oyarzabal, with 5 goals and 2 assists, is in fine form and will be a major danger.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Osasuna, sitting 17th with 11 points, have struggled offensively, scoring just 9 goals (19th in the league), though their defense is relatively solid (5th fewest goals conceded). Real Sociedad, positioned 14th with 13 points, have a slightly better attack (14 goals) and have shown resilience with a recent unbeaten streak, making them slight favorites.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore’s prediction of a 41% chance for a home win, 34% for a draw, and 25% for an away win, combined with Real Sociedad’s superior recent form and head-to-head dominance, this match is likely to be closely contested. However, Osasuna’s home advantage at Estadio El Sadar and desperation for points could push them to a narrow victory or at least a draw.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Osasuna leads Real Sociedad 1-0 at Estadio El Sadar in Pamplona. The home side took the lead just before the break with a headed goal by Alejandro Catena in the 42nd minute. Despite Real Sociedad holding more possession, Osasuna capitalized on a key opportunity to secure the advantage. Both teams showed intent in the first half, but Osasuna’s clinical finishing made the difference.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance
halftimeSections.text: Osasuna displayed a solid defensive structure while being effective on the counter. Their ability to create a big chance and convert it highlights their efficiency in attack. Real Sociedad, on the other hand, enjoyed greater control of the ball and made more entries into the penalty area, yet they failed to create any significant scoring opportunities. Their expected goals were notably lower, reflecting a lack of cutting edge in the final third. Osasuna’s aerial dominance and defensive solidity have given them an edge so far.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players
halftimeSections.text: Alejandro Catena has been the standout for Osasuna, not only scoring the decisive goal but also contributing defensively with clearances and interceptions. Aimar Oroz also played a crucial role with an assist, showing creativity in midfield. For Real Sociedad, players like Arsen Zakharyan and Jon Aramburu showed glimpses of promise with shots on target and key passes, but they couldn’t break through Osasuna’s defense. Real Sociedad will need their attacking players to step up in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides. Osasuna started with strong attacking intent, pushing forward in the opening minutes. Real Sociedad responded with a prolonged period of dominance in the middle of the half, sustaining pressure and creating several attacking spells. However, Osasuna regained control towards the end of the half, culminating in their goal. This shift late in the half could give them a psychological boost heading into the second period.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: Real Sociedad are likely to come out aggressively in the second half as they chase an equalizer. Their higher possession and penalty area entries suggest they have the potential to create chances if they can improve their finishing. Osasuna, buoyed by their lead, might adopt a more cautious approach, looking to protect their advantage while exploiting counter-attacking opportunities. The game remains finely poised, and tactical adjustments at the break could play a significant role.
